title: crepidiastrum denticulatum subsp. ramosissimum
intro: Crepidiastrum denticulatum subsp. ramosissimum, also known as Korean dodonaea, is a subspecies of plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema. The health benefits of Korean dodonaea include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are taken orally or applied topically to the affected area.
